job_description:
Sr. B2B Content Specialist

2Base Technologies is at a deliberate stage of building its marketing function, and this role owns the written voice of that function end to end. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

This is a senior writing role with a specific, demanding purpose. It is not a content management role or a social media role. If that distinction is unclear from the outset, this is probably not the right fit.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not invent the voice. It operationalises, applies, and enforces it across all written output: - Long-form content that earns search traffic - Content written in the voice of senior leadership for professional publishing - The quality standard for everything written that leaves this company

The audience spans technical leaders and business decision-makers at mid-market companies: CTOs, IT Directors, Founders, COOs, and Operations leaders. Writing that works across this range, without oversimplifying for one end or losing the other, is the actual craft challenge of this role. Technically credible, commercially relevant, and written with a clear point of view. That is the standard this role is hired to meet and maintain.

What You Will Own

  • All long-form written content: pillar pages, blogs, playbooks, decision frameworks, and use-case libraries targeting senior decision-makers in the US and UK
  • Written content produced in the voice of senior leadership for professional platforms
  • The 2Base content voice, including auditing all published content to ensure brand standards
  • Content briefs written clearly enough that a junior team member can produce a first draft without needing clarifications
  • The content quality gate: nothing is published without passing through this role first
  • AI tool usage as a productivity layer, not as a substitute for thinking

What You Will Not Own

  • SEO keyword selection (handled by the SEO Specialist)
  • Social media scheduling and publishing (handled by a separate team member)
  • Visual design of content assets (handled by the Visual Designer)
  • Distribution strategy (handled by the Marketing & Growth Lead)
  • ICP definition (you write for the ICP, you do not define it)

Requirements

Experience

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years writing B2B content, with strong candidates typically at the 5 to 6 year mark
  • Has written content that ranked on Google, not just content that was published
  • Has written in voices other than their own, for executives, founders, or senior professionals
  • Understands SEO at a working level: knows what search intent means and can write to it without being an SEO specialist
  • Has worked with AI writing tools and knows both their value and their limits
  • Can show content that contributed to a measurable outcome: traffic, leads, or engagement with the right audience

Background

  • Technology, SaaS, or professional services background strongly preferred

Writing Ability

  • Writes with a clear point of view, not neutral summaries of other people's ideas
  • Can explain complex technical concepts in plain language without losing technical credibility
  • Writes concisely with no filler, no padding, and no unnecessary qualification
  • Can write in different voices and make each one sound authentic

Mindset

  • Treats content as a commercial tool, not a creative exercise
  • Comfortable being edited and does not treat feedback as personal criticism
  • Genuinely curious about AI, technology, and business operations
  • Fast: this team moves at pace and cannot wait three days for a first draft

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios of "Top 10 Tips" articles or trend summaries with no original thinking
  • Writers who cannot explain how any of their content performed or what it contributed
  • Anyone who has never written for a technical or senior business audience
  • Writers who only produce self-directed content and have never worked consistently to a brief
  • Writers who cannot write convincingly in a voice other than their own
  • Candidates who treat AI tools as the primary author and apply only light editing

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Senior B2B Content Specialist required to operationalize and enforce 2Base's written voice across technical and business audiences. Focus on long-form content, leadership voice writing, and AI tool integration for enterprise clients.

job_description:
Sr. Visual Designer 2Base Technologies

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from scratch, and this role makes it look like a company worth taking seriously. We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

Before a CTO reads a word of our content, they see the design. Before they trust the argument, they have already made a judgment about whether this company looks credible. This role owns that first impression and every asset that follows.

The visual function at 2Base is being built from the ground up. There is no brand system to inherit, no template library to use, and no consistent visual identity to maintain. What exists is a clear strategic direction, a defined audience of senior technology and operations leaders, and a marketing team building the content engine around it. Your job is to give that engine a visual identity it deserves.

The first 30 days of this role have one priority: build the brand system. Everything else, including LinkedIn carousels, ad creatives, landing pages, and presentation templates, is built from that foundation. A designer who wants to jump straight into production before the system exists is not the right fit.

This is not a creative agency role. The audience is sceptical, senior, and technically literate. Design that looks clever but reads as unclear, or feels consumer-facing rather than professional, will not work with this ICP. Restraint, precision, and consistency matter more than visual novelty.

What You Will Own

  • The 2Base visual brand system, including colour palette, typography, spacing, layout rules, and documentation
  • Marketing and content visual assets (LinkedIn carousels, social graphics, infographics, diagrams)
  • Paid advertising creatives (Google Display, LinkedIn Ads) designed for testing and iteration
  • Landing page visual design (layout, components, conversion-focused structure)
  • Email, presentation, and sales deck templates
  • A template library enabling team members to produce on-brand assets independently
  • Design consistency auditing (periodic review of published assets)
  • Secondary responsibility: Light video editing and motion for social reels, YouTube, and website use

What You Will Not Own

  • What an asset communicates (owned by the Content and Thought Leadership Specialist)
  • Copywriting (owned by the Content Specialist)
  • Website development (owned by the development team)
  • Content strategy or brand positioning

Requirements

Experience - Minimum 3 to 5 years in a B2B design role (technology, SaaS, or professional services preferred) - Experience building or evolving a brand system - Experience designing for LinkedIn (carousels, document posts, single-image feed content) - Experience creating paid ad creatives that have been executed, tested, and iterated - Experience designing conversion-focused landing pages - Comfort with basic video editing for social and web content

Proficiency - Figma (non-negotiable) - Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, Premiere Pro, After Effects)

Design Thinking - Portfolio demonstrates restraint through clean, structured, and purposeful design - Ability to explain reasoning behind key design decisions - Designs tailored to a senior B2B audience - Strong typography skills

Mindset - Works effectively from structured briefs - Maintains speed without compromising quality - Treats design as a communication tool - Open to feedback and constructive critique

What We Are Not Looking For

  • Portfolios dominated by consumer brands, lifestyle work, event design, or non-B2B campaigns
  • Candidates who cannot justify design decisions
  • Candidates without brand system-building experience
  • Designers who overcomplicate visuals with excessive fonts, colours, styles, or effects
  • Candidates requiring complete creative freedom and struggling with structured briefs

Benefits

  • Growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Lead 2Base Technologies' visual brand system and design strategy for B2B marketing assets. Requires 3-5 years of B2B design experience with expertise in Figma and brand system development.

job_description:
QA Engineer

We are seeking a detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years of hands-on experience in software testing to join our QA team. In this role, you will ensure the quality and reliability of web and mobile applications by participating in test planning, test case creation, test execution, and defect tracking.

Key Responsibilities: - Review BRD, SRS, user stories, and functional specifications to understand system requirements. - Translate requirements into structured test scenarios and documented test cases for comprehensive coverage. - Perform functional, regression, and exploratory testing across browsers, devices, and environments. - Identify edge cases, alternate flows, and boundary conditions to validate system behavior. - Log, document, and track defects with clear reproduction steps and severity classification. - Validate bug fixes and perform regression testing to maintain stability during releases. - Maintain test documentation, reports, and QA artifacts for traceability and quality tracking. - Support test summaries, progress updates, and QA reports during development cycles. - Collaborate with business analysts, developers, project managers, and senior QA engineers. - Participate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (sprint planning, stand-ups, reviews). - Perform basic API validation and data verification for integration-level testing. - Own assigned modules/features to ensure testing completeness and release readiness. - Improve test coverage, testing approaches, and QA processes within the team. - Test AI-powered/data-driven features by validating output accuracy and system behavior. - Learn automation fundamentals and emerging QA tools, including AI testing practices.

Required Skills & Mindset: - Strong attention to detail and structured thinking. - Willingness to understand business logic before testing. - Ability to identify edge cases and risks beyond happy paths. - Analytical mindset to question unexpected system behavior. - Clear communication and collaboration skills. - Solid documentation skills for defect reporting and test case writing. - Ability to work independently in fast-paced environments. - Understanding of functional, regression, integration, and exploratory testing. - Experience with test case design techniques. - Proficiency in bug tracking and test management tools. - Basic API testing experience (Postman, REST Assured). - Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies and QA’s role in sprints. - Knowledge of AI-assisted testing.

Good to Have: - Basic awareness of performance and security testing. - Interest in test automation frameworks (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress). - Scripting knowledge in Python, JavaScript, or Java. - Exposure to AI-enabled applications (chatbots, recommendation engines). - Awareness of modern QA practices (continuous testing, CI/CD). - Ability to use AI-assisted tools for test case generation and data preparation. - Experience with mobile testing frameworks (Appium). - Risk-based testing prioritization. - Commitment to continuous learning and QA tool/methodology updates. - ISTQB certification or equivalent.

Experience & Education: - 1–3 years of experience. - B.Tech in IT, CS, or MCA.

Contact: For inquiries, email your resume to careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Detail-oriented Associate QA Engineer with 1–3 years experience in manual testing, defect tracking, and Agile/Scrum. Collaborates with teams to ensure quality in web and mobile applications using QA tools.

job_description:
Sr. Business Analyst

At 2Base, we are rebuilding the Business Analyst function into a world-class, product-minded, clarity-driven team. This role is not a documentation role. It is not a screen updater role. It is not a support role. This is a core Business Analyst position responsible for functional modelling, system reasoning, requirement clarity, and building the foundation for correct development.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead requirement workshops, client calls, and discovery discussions to understand business needs clearly.
  • Ask deeper questions, challenge assumptions, and validate the true business intent behind every requirement.
  • Convert ambiguous client inputs into structured, logical, and actionable functional understanding.
  • Break large features into modules, workflows, components, and clear dependencies.
  • Build strong system-level understanding involving APIs, integrations, data flow, and backend considerations.
  • Translate business processes into functional logic that development teams can execute.
  • Decompose requirements into use-case flows, system behaviours, edge cases, alternate paths, validation rules, and dependencies.
  • Prepare high-quality documentation, including BRD, FRD, SRS, workflows, state diagrams, data-flow diagrams, API requirement specs, and acceptance criteria.
  • Ensure all documentation is clear, logical, structured, testable, and ready for developers and QA to use.
  • Collaborate closely with developers, architects, QA, and product owners to validate feasibility and align understanding.
  • Ensure QA teams can derive test cases effectively from your functional logic and acceptance criteria.
  • Communicate confidently with clients, handle requirement discussions smoothly, and clarify misunderstandings immediately.
  • Push back politely on unclear or changing requirements and ensure final decisions are documented properly.
  • Maintain requirement traceability throughout the lifecycle and ensure zero ambiguity during handover to development.
  • Own the entire requirement lifecycle from discovery to development readiness with high accountability.
  • Drive clarity across teams by ensuring every requirement is complete, validated, and logically consistent.

Required Skills & Experience

  • Minimum 4+ years of core Business Analyst experience with strong analytical and system-level thinking.

It will be your responsibility to design, implement, and execute test cases, identify, and document defects, and work closely with the development team to ensure the timely delivery of high-quality software.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Analyze business and technical requirements to create test plans and test cases.
  • Develop comprehensive test plans in collaboration with project delivery team, business analyst, developers, and other stakeholders. Test cases should be defined and prioritized based on the project's goals, specifications, and risks.
  • Liaison with development during the pre-testing phase, including review of requirements and development specifications, to support test planning and preparation.
  • Ensure that software applications are functional, usable, and performant by executing test cases. Perform manual and automated testing as necessary. Test results, including defect reports and test summaries, should be documented, and communicated.
  • Set-up and maintenance of test environments in liaison with technical staff and development teams.
  • The identification, isolation, and documentation of defects should be done in a clear and concise manner. Ensure that bugs are resolved in a timely manner by collaborating with developers and retesting them after they have been fixed.
  • Assist in the development and maintenance of automated test scripts. Work towards increasing automated test coverage by identifying opportunities for test automation.
  • Parallel execution of multiple test projects.
  • Regression testing should be conducted to ensure that new changes do not adversely affect existing functionality.
  • Work closely with the development team to ensure that the software architecture and design are understood in order to facilitate effective testing. Ensure the project's success by collaborating with cross-functional teams.
  • Meeting software quality targets as determined by the IT Projects Delivery Manager
  • Liaise with customers and Project Management during UAT, ensuring that issues are replicated and resolved in a timely manner.
  • Preparation of test reports, including supporting hand-over notes at the conclusion of the test project.
  • To be aware of business continuity processes via the line manager.
  • Keep abreast of industry best practices, testing tools, and technologies. Improve testing processes and methodologies by proposing and implementing improvements.

Contact:

Kindly share resume at career@pcsindia.email.
summary: The QA Analyst ensures software quality by designing test cases, identifying defects, and collaborating with development teams. Responsibilities include manual and automated testing, defect tracking, and maintaining test environments.

job_description:
User Acquisition Specialist

We are seeking a highly motivated and data-driven User Acquisition Specialist to join our growth team. You will be responsible for managing the entire UA funnel — from campaign conceptualization to in-depth performance analysis — with the goal of acquiring high-quality users at scale and maximizing long-term ROI. This role requires a mix of technical expertise, strategic thinking, analytical rigor, and creative collaboration, making it ideal for someone passionate about scaling mobile games in the social casino space.

Key Responsibilities

  1. Technical & Execution
  2. Plan, launch, and optimize UA campaigns across Meta (Facebook/Instagram), Google Ads (UAC), TikTok, Apple Search Ads, Unity, and other ad networks/DSPs.
  3. Manage daily campaign performance, adjusting bids, budgets, targeting, and creatives to hit CAC, LTV, and ROI goals.
  4. Implement structured A/B tests for campaigns, audiences, and creatives.
  5. Ensure accurate event configuration and tracking across ad platforms and Mobile Measurement Partners (MMPs) (AppsFlyer).

  6. Partner with engineering teams to QA and troubleshoot SDK/event integrations.

  7. Analytical & Reporting

  8. Analyze campaign and cohort-level data from ad platforms and MMPs to identify trends, opportunities, and risks.
  9. Calculate breakeven points and model projected LTV/ROI by user cohorts.
  10. Understand and act on player journey metrics (e.g., New users: D0–D7; Retained users: D7+; Reactivated users).
  11. Build dashboards and performance reports in tools such as Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI.

  12. Apply predictive modeling and early KPIs (D0–D3) to forecast long-term user value, for decision making.

  13. Strategic

  14. Develop and execute a full-funnel UA strategy that integrates acquisition, retention, and re-engagement.
  15. Create and manage audience strategies (custom audiences, lookalikes, retargeting cohorts).
  16. Design and execute re-engagement campaigns for churned or low-activity users.
  17. Collaborate with CRM and product teams to connect UA with retention and monetization strategies.

  18. Stay current with platform updates, privacy-first UA (SKAN, ATT, Privacy Sandbox), and emerging ad tech.

  19. Creative

  20. Collaborate with the creative team to brief, conceptualize, and test ad creatives (video, playables, static, UGC-style).
  21. Design and run creative test campaigns with structured methodologies to minimize false positives/false negatives.
  22. Analyze creative performance using key metrics (Hook Rate, Hold Rate, CTR, CVR, ROAS etc.) and translate findings into actionable creative insights.
  23. Build data-driven audience categories and ensure visual assets + ad copy are tailored to the right segment.
  24. Establish creative refresh cycles and rotation strategies to combat ad fatigue.

  25. Share business-aligned creative insights with stakeholders, linking creative learnings to broader UA strategy.

  26. Collaboration & Operations

  27. Act as the primary liaison with ad networks, agencies, and internal cross-functional teams (Analytics, Engineering, Product, Creative).
  28. Lead the day-to-day campaign operations, ensuring accuracy, quality, and timeliness.
  29. Share insights and recommendations with stakeholders to influence product and marketing decisions.

Qualifications

  • 4+ years of hands-on experience in UA for mobile games (social, hypercasual, or casino preferred).
  • Proven expertise with major ad platforms (Meta, Google Ads, TikTok, ASA).
  • Strong working knowledge of MMPs (AppsFlyer, Adjust, Singular) including event setup and analysis.
  • Deep understanding of UA KPIs (CPI, CAC, ROAS, ROI, LTV, retention).
  • Ability to run re-engagement campaigns and leverage MMP + platform data for targeting and predictive analysis.
  • Proficiency in data analysis & visualization tools (Excel, SQL, Looker Studio, Tableau, or equivalent).
  • Strong analytical, mathematical, and problem-solving skills.
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a high sense of ownership and accountability.

Send the CV to careersindia@rubyseven.com. If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careersindia@rubyseven.com.
